member.name: Rebecca Harris
member.synopsis: Rebecca Harris is the Conservative MP for Castle Point , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010. She currently undertakes the role of Opposition Chief Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Official Cars
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the breakdown of the Government Car Service operational fleet is by vehicle (a) make and (b) model; and what proportion are manufactured by British manufacturers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Car Service does not disclose the make and model of vehicles within its operational fleet, as releasing this information could compromise the security arrangements of Ministers.

writtenQuestions.heading: Diplomatic Service: Official Cars
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, the breakdown of (a) make and (b) type of cars used by (i) ambassadors and (ii) embassy officials at British embassies; and what proportion are manufactured by British manufa
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 8 December 2025 in response to Question 96748.

writtenQuestions.heading: National Highways: Motor Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the breakdown of the National Highways operational fleet is by vehicle (a) make and (b) model; and what proportion are manufactured by British manufacturers.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sodium Valproate and Surgical Mesh Implants: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to respond to the recommendations of the Hughes Report, Options for redress for those harmed by valproate and pelvic mesh, published on 7 February 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is carefully considering the valuable work done by the Hughes Report, and will respond as soon as possible.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Working Hours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health, what assessment he has made of the effect of changes to the supplementary hours worked by pharmacists on provision of pharmaceutical services in each NHS locality.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The community pharmacy in 2016/17 and beyond reforms announced on 20 October were considered in respect of the public sector equality duty, the family test and relevant duties of the Secretary of State under the National Health Service Act 2006. This incl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Liquefied Petroleum Gas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what plans his Department has to encourage the take-up of liquid petroleum gas conversions by taxis and vans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport (DfT), working with the Department for Environment Food and Rural Affairs, have evaluated a range of options for tackling poor air quality; this formed the basis of the Government’s draft air quality plans that recently went t...

writtenQuestions.heading: EU Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign and Commonwealth Affairs, what assessment he has made of the progress of negotiations to reform the EU and the UK's relationship with the EU.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Prime Minister, my right hon. Friend the Member for Witney (Mr Cameron) has set out substantial reforms in four key areas to fix the problems in the UK's relationship with the EU. Technical talks have been ongoing since July. The Prime Minister update...

writtenQuestions.heading: Legal Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for International Development, how much her Department has spent on legal fees in the case of The Queen (on the application of O) and the Secretary of State for International Development.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are unable to comment on issues relating to an ongoing legal action.

writtenQuestions.heading: Legal Aid Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how much in legal aid claims was paid to Leigh Day in 2013-14.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The net payment to Leigh Day in the 2013/14 financial year was £529,559.30. This payment covers all work undertaken by the firm under legal aid. The payment made will be offset by recoupment on successful cases where the opponent has paid the costs. Leg...

writtenQuestions.heading: Higher Education: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills, what assessment his Department has made of the effect of the reduction in the student opportunity allocation on the ability of financially-disadvantaged students to access higher education
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the annual grant letters to the Higher Education Funding Council for England, the Government has made it clear that widening participation is a priority. Overall investment in widening participation through the Student Opportunity Fund and other source...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving: Eyesight
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if he will bring forward proposals to introduce compulsory testing to ensure that drivers' eyesight meets minimum legal standards.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no plans to introduce compulsory eyesight tests for drivers. All drivers are required by law to ensure that they are able to read a number plate from 20 metres and have a visual acuity of at least 6/12 (decimal 0.5), as measured on the Snellen c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Met Office
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills, whether the new Met Office supercomputer will be better able to predict heavy storms such as the one in Castle Point on 20 July 2014; and whether that supercomputer will enable agencies li
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new supercomputer will enable the Met Office to predict small-scale, high impact weather features – such as thunderstorms – with greater detail and accuracy, and to provide earlier warnings of severe weather to emergency responders.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many patients in the UK were diagnosed with secondary brain tumours in (a) 2008, (b) 2009, (c) 2010, (d) 2011, (e) 2012 and (f) 2013.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls within the responsibility of the UK Statistics Authority. I have asked the Authority to reply.

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many patients in the UK were diagnosed with high grade brain tumours in (a) 2008, (b) 2009, (c) 2010, (d) 2011, (e) 2012 and (f) 2013.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls within the responsibility of the UK Statistics Authority. I have asked the Authority to reply.

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many patients in the UK were diagnosed with low grade brain tumours in (a) 2008, (b) 2009, (c) 2010, (d) 2011, (e) 2012 and (f) 2013.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls within the responsibility of the UK Statistics Authority. I have asked the Authority to reply.
